mission: Reduce the non-live APS/anomaly-inflow route to its exact future datum and determine whether any hidden inflow object remains on the current closed-horizon stack.

theorem_1_inflow_requires_odd_bulk_and_b.statement: A genuine boundary eta-shift from APS/anomaly inflow requires more than a closed 2D boundary carrier. It requires a 3D bulk/cobordism datum Y with boundary S^2 together with a parity-odd action or transport law whose variation couples to the boundary fermionic sector.

formulas.boundary_effect: delta eta_boundary != 0 only through parity-odd bulk coupling

theorem_2_current_stack_has_no_inflow_pa.statement: The present stack derives no open boundary end, no 3D parity-odd action, and no bulk-to-boundary eta-shift law. Therefore the minimal inflow pair (Y, kappa_odd) is absent on the current stack.

imports.aps_boundary: APS/anomaly inflow is not a live route on the current stack. The admitted horizon carrier is a closed S^2 problem with no derived open boundary end and no derived 3D parity-odd Chern-Simons functional whose variation would generate a boundary eta-shift. So anomaly inflow is not currently hidden in the existing IO data; it would require a genuinely new 3D parity-odd boundary extension.

theorem_3_exact_future_inflow_target.statement: The APS/anomaly-inflow route is therefore reduced to one exact future object class: a parity-odd 3D extension datum (Y, kappa_odd) that induces a nonzero boundary eta shift. Without that pair, no inflow-based Paper 35 closure can occur.

final_boundary.safe_statement: The APS/anomaly-inflow route is fully reduced. On the current stack there is no hidden inflow object. Any future positive route must explicitly derive a parity-odd 3D extension pair (Y, kappa_odd) producing a nonzero boundary eta shift.
